response: Originating as a US military project in the 1960s, the internet's modern foundation was built upon the adoption of TCP/IP in 1983 and the invention of the World Wide Web in 1989. The 1993 launch of the Mosaic web browser made the internet publicly accessible, leading to a commercial boom, the rise of social media, and mobile access. This evolution has resulted in a global network that now connects over 5 billion people and underpins nearly every aspect of modern life.
